violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to register an employee with food safety training with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Baby formula was observed held past its disposition date. Food items shall be disposed of prior to their disposition date. Items were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The 3-bay sink located in the produce prep area was observed visibly rusted and was not in good repair. Establishment is to clean or replace sink so that it is in good repair and free of potential contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the meat department walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
The floor tiles in the produce prep area were observed cracked or missing and were in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per interview, renewal was submitted to RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Bulk baked goods in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with a list of ingredients for consumer viewing. PIC indicated that establishment would put together binder of ingredients and place behind the bakery counter for viewing upon customer request. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
Walk-in door gasket for the seafood department walk-in was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

The receptacle in both toilet rooms is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The floor in the produce cooler is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2024

Protective shielding was cracked for the lights in the meat department walk-in. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
The hand-washing sink in the meat department was blocked by recycling receptacle. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

The Deli display case was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit, items were observed at temperatures between 42 and 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. The deli display case was evaluated and items that were out of temperature control were voluntarily disposed of at time of inspection. Thermometers were calibrated at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
Log of bologna was observed past the sell by date on item packaging. Item was voluntarily disposed of at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the shellfish display case that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Leftover chicken was placed in walk-in cooler with bags closed and condensate was observed on inside of packaging. Proper cooling methods must be developed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2024
Thermometer provided for deli display case was observed to be inaccurate. Must install accurate thermometer in unit. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024

Bulk baked goods in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with a list of ingredients for consumer viewing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
walk-in door gasket for the seafood department walk-in was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024
The receptacle in both toilet rooms is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
The floor in the produce cooler is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/20/2024

Protective shielding was cracked for the lights in the meat department walk-in. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024
